title: townsendia glabella
intro: Townsendia glabella, also known as the white prairie clover, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the western regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Townsendia glabella include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and provide antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water, and it is often used in teas, salves, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
